vocab.meaning: xả cơn giận/giải tỏa áp lực
vocab.example: To avoid getting frustrated during gridlock, I sing along to loud music just to let off steam.

vocab.meaning: thả hồn treo ngược cành cây/mải mê suy nghĩ về chuyện khác
vocab.example: When the traffic is at a standstill, I often find myself being miles away, daydreaming about my next vacation.

vocab.meaning: cắn răng chịu đựng một tình huống khó chịu
vocab.example: There's no escape from the rush hour in this city, so I just have to bite the bullet and wait it out.

vocab.meaning: trong cái rủi có cái may
vocab.example: Surprisingly, the traffic jam was a blessing in disguise as it gave me extra time to finish my audiobook.

vocab.word: at the end of one's tether
vocab.meaning: kiệt sức và mất sạch kiên nhẫn
vocab.example: By the time I finally got home after two hours in traffic, I was truly at the end of my tether.

vocab.meaning: chỉnh đốn lại cách làm việc/chuẩn bị kỹ lưỡng
vocab.example: I use the delay to get my act together by mentally reviewing my talking points for the upcoming presentation.
In the middle of such a massive jam, it’s easy to lose your bearings and forget which turn-off you were supposed to take.

vocab.meaning: biết ơn vì những gì mình có (thay vì phàn nàn)
vocab.example: Instead of fuming, I count my blessings that I'm in a comfortable car with air conditioning and not out in the rain.

vocab.meaning: nhìn nhận mọi việc một cách khách quan/không trầm trọng hóa vấn đề
vocab.example: I try to keep things in perspective; being ten minutes late isn't the end of the world, even if the traffic is abysmal.

vocab.meaning: làm ngay lập tức/quyết định nhanh trong lúc đang thực hiện việc khác
vocab.example: If the main road is blocked, I have to find an alternative route on the fly using my navigation app.
